# nayatel_security_group (Resource)

Manages a Nayatel Cloud security group with rules.

## Example Usage

```hcl
resource "nayatel_security_group" "web" {
  name        = "web-servers"
  description = "Security group for web servers"

  rule {
    direction   = "ingress"
    protocol    = "tcp"
    port_number = "80"
    cidr        = "0.0.0.0/0"
  }

  rule {
    direction   = "ingress"
    protocol    = "tcp"
    port_number = "443"
    cidr        = "0.0.0.0/0"
  }

  rule {
    direction   = "ingress"
    protocol    = "tcp"
    port_number = "22"
    cidr        = "10.0.0.0/8"
  }
}
```

**Note:** Changing any rule will recreate the entire security group since the API does not support deleting individual rules.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the security group (API may add a suffix)

### Optional

- `description` (String) Description of the security group
- `rule` (Block List) Security group rules (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rule))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Security group identifier

<a id="nestedblock--rule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rule`

Required:

- `direction` (String) Direction: 'ingress' or 'egress'

Optional:

- `cidr` (String) CIDR block. Defaults to '0.0.0.0/0'
- `ethertype` (String) Ethertype: 'IPv4' or 'IPv6'. Defaults to 'IPv4'
- `port_number` (String) Port number (e.g., '80', '443', '22')
- `protocol` (String) Protocol: 'tcp', 'udp', 'icmp'. Defaults to 'tcp'
